title: cistus polymorphus var. corsicus
intro: Cistus polymorphus var. corsicus, commonly known as the Sardinian rockrose, is a plant species belonging to the Cistus genus, characterized by its pink to white flowers and evergreen leaves. The Sardinian rockrose has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable treatment for wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cistus polymorphus var. corsicus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
